MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. MATTHEW S. DA VEGA OF THE LAW FIRM OF BRAYTON & PURCELL, LLP, TEL. NO. 898-1555 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LISA L. OBERG OF THE LAW FIRM OF MCKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE, TEL. NO. 267-4175 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO EXPOSURE THROUGH HIS DECLARATION CONCERNING USE OF THE TUNNEL DURING CONSTRUCTION AND THE TESTIMONY OF AY AND COHEN CONCERNING ASBESTOS DUST GENERATED BY INSTALLATION OF UNIBESTOS, AND ITS CONTAMINATION OF THE TUNNEL. DEFENDANT HAS NOT CARRIED ITS BURDEN ON THE GOVERNMENT CONTRACTOR DEFENSE. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR# 8189 =301/PJB/BD
